subprocess_flow.description: The project team aggregates all mandatory compliance artifacts into a single, structured dossier for board review. This includes the final Audit Certification, the signed-off DPIA Report, the Risk Mitigation Plan, and technical security documentation to ensure the board has a holistic view of the system's risk posture.
success_criteria: All mandatory documents are present and correctly formatted.
Dossier is uploaded to the central governance portal.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To centralize all evidence required for the formal approval decision.
end_conditions: Dossier finalized and ready for review

step_risks_and_controls.risk: Incomplete evidence leading to board rejection.
step_risks_and_controls.control: Mandatory checklist validation prior to submission.

change_management.versioning_rules: Dossier versioning linked to project ID.

subprocess_flow.description: The Chief Data Officer or a designated governance lead performs a preliminary review of the dossier to ensure it meets the necessary quality and compliance thresholds. This step acts as a filter to prevent incomplete or low-quality submissions from reaching the Governance Board, thereby optimizing board time.
success_criteria: CDO confirms dossier readiness for board presentation.
No critical gaps identified in the evidence.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To ensure the quality and completeness of the submission before formal board review.
subprocess_flow.scope: Internal review of the evidence dossier.
triggers: Dossier submission to the Data Office

step_risks_and_controls.control: Use of a standardized QA rubric.

subprocess_flow.description: The project lead and CDO present the AI system's compliance posture, ethical considerations, and risk mitigation strategies to the MoJ Governance Board. This session allows board members to ask clarifying questions and scrutinize the evidence provided in the dossier.
success_criteria: All board queries addressed or logged for follow-up.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To provide the board with the necessary context to make an informed approval decision.

step_risks_and_controls.control: Advance scheduling and mandatory attendance tracking.

subprocess_flow.description: Following a positive board decision, the CDO formally signs and issues the Authorization to Operate (AtO). This document serves as the official permit for the AI system to transition into the live production environment, signifying that all governance requirements have been met.
success_criteria: AtO is signed by the CDO.
AtO is distributed to the project team and DPO.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To grant formal organizational approval for live operation.

step_risks_and_controls.control: Digital signature and secure document storage.

subprocess_flow.description: The final step involves updating the MoJ's central AI model inventory to reflect the system's new 'Authorized' status. This includes uploading the AtO and linking it to the project's registration record to maintain a complete audit trail for the department.
success_criteria: AtO document is accessible via the central catalog.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To maintain an accurate and up-to-date record of all authorized AI systems.
subprocess_flow.scope: Data entry and record management in the central inventory.

step_risks_and_controls.control: Automated synchronization with the governance portal.
